body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: yes

## Scope of this cell

This is the `model-switch` cell — the **interactive one-shot `/model <name>` slash switch**. Aider exposes the swap as a single in-chat command (unlike codex's two-step interactive picker driven with `keys`, or claudecode's arrow-key TUI picker). The aider recipe sends `/model openai/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored` between turn 1 and turn 2. It shares the `model-switch-midsession` coverage_id with the sibling cell, so the observability question is the same; this artifact records it for the interactive-slash framing specifically.

## Reasoning

### Agent supports it

Aider's `/model <name>` is a first-class in-chat slash command — `cmd_model` in `aider/commands.py:87` is documented as "Switch the Main Model to a new LLM" and raises `SwitchCoder(main_model=…)` to replace the active main model for all subsequent turns (verified in the installed aider 0.86.2 package, `aider/__init__.py` `__version__ = "0.86.2"`). The official command reference lists the same (https://aider.chat/docs/usage/commands.html). A subsequent turn then runs on the new model — exactly the `multi_model_orchestration` primitive ("per-turn model selection — agent can change models between turns," `replaydata/agents/aider/capabilities.json` → `multi_model_orchestration: true`). The recipe's switch target (`openai/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored`) is a one-shot inline argument, so no picker UI / `keys` step is needed.

### irrlicht observes it

The scenario's three user-observable signals — `ModelName = <model A>` after turn 1, `ModelName = <model B>` after turn 2, and `TotalTokens(t2) > TotalTokens(t1)` — all fall out of the aider parser + tailer as they exist today:

- When the `/model` switch lands, the new coder re-emits its announcements and aider writes a `> Main model: <name> …` blockquote to `.aider.chat.history.md`. The parser's `modelRE` (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/aider/parser.go:66`) matches **both** `> Model:` and `> Main model:`, updates `p.model`, and emits a `Skip` event carrying the new `ModelName` (parser.go:110-113).
- Each model call closes on a `> Tokens: …` line, and `closeModelCall` (parser.go:153) stamps the current `p.model` onto the `assistant_message` event's `ModelName` and `Contribution.Model`, so turn 2's usage is attributed to model B.
- `TranscriptTailer.applyModelMetadata` (`core/pkg/tailer/tailer_metrics.go:105`) sets `t.metrics.ModelName` on any event with a non-empty `ModelName` — latest-wins, no first-model latch — flowing to `session.ModelName` and the dashboard. The merge guard at `core/domain/session/session.go:653` only *retains* the old model when the new one is empty/`unknown`; a real model-B name is never blocked, so the inter-turn switch refreshes the displayed `ModelName`.
- Token snapshots accumulate cumulatively across turns regardless of model, so `TotalTokens` after turn 2 strictly exceeds turn 1.

This is pinned by a dedicated regression test, `TestParser_MainModel_AfterSlashCommand` (`parser_test.go:214`), which exercises the exact `> Main model: openai/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored …` line this recipe produces and asserts the post-switch turn carries the new model — its comment states it exists specifically so "model_changed scenarios silently fail because post-switch turns keep the original model name on Contribution.Model" cannot happen.

Transport is `FilesUnderCWD{ .aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser }` (`agent.go:45`) — a real local transcript exists in each project CWD, so there is no transport gap of the kind that blocks cloud-only scenarios.

### Matrix drift

The coverage cell (keyed `model-switch-midsession`, shared by both cells) records `irrlicht_observes: partial` with the note "architect/editor pair detected per turn but inter-turn switch may not refresh." That concern is contradicted by both the `latest-wins` code path above and the dedicated regression test that exists precisely to guarantee the post-switch refresh. The honest verdict is `yes`; the maintainer should update the coverage row from `partial` to `yes`. (The sibling `aider/model-switch-midsession` assessment already reached the same `yes/yes` conclusion.)

## Confidence

0.85: the `/model` command is documented by the vendor and confirmed in the installed 0.86.2 source (`cmd_model`), and the observability claim is grounded directly in the adapter source plus a pinning unit test that uses this recipe's exact switch line. Held below 0.9 because the verdict is confirmed at the parser/unit level rather than by an end-to-end recording of a live `/model` swap (no recording exists for this cell yet — that's Stage 4's job).
caveats: Aider does not emit per-turn cost in a form the adapter tracks (capabilities `cost_tracking: false`); model name and token counts refresh across the switch, but per-model cost attribution is neither asserted by this scenario nor tracked.

feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant: the `> Weak model: …` line aider may also print is intentionally NOT matched by `modelRE` (parser.go:64-66) — only `Model:` / `Main model:` drive `ModelName`. Correct for this scenario (the weak/summary model is not the turn's main model), but a switch that changed only the weak model would be invisible.

sources.note: official in-chat command reference: `/model` = "Switch the Main Model to a new LLM"; `/editor-model`, `/architect` also listed
cmd_model (L87) "Switch the Main Model to a new LLM" raises SwitchCoder(main_model=…) on a one-shot `/model <name>` arg — interactive slash, no picker; installed aider 0.86.2

modelRE (L66) matches `> Main model: …` (the line the new coder emits after a /model switch) identically to `> Model:`, updates p.model, emits Skip+ModelName (L110); closeModelCall (L153) stamps p.model onto each assistant_message ModelName + Contribution.Model
TestParser_MainModel_AfterSlashCommand (L214) drives the exact `> Main model: openai/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored …` line this recipe produces and asserts the post-switch turn carries the new model
Source = FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser} (L45) — a real local transcript exists per project CWD; no cloud transport gap
applyModelMetadata (L105) sets t.metrics.ModelName on any event with a non-empty ModelName — latest-wins, no first-model latch; flows to session.ModelName → dashboard
merge guard L653 only retains old ModelName when the new one is empty/unknown; a valid model-B name is never blocked, so the inter-turn switch refreshes
multi_model_orchestration: true (the primitive this scenario exercises); cost_tracking: false (basis for caveat 1)
